---
title: "Cloud Modernization Validation Suite"
subtitle: "Conformance tests and validation criteria for the cloud domain"
doc-type: domain-validation-suite
audience: [Customer, Assessor, Adapter Owner]
classification: Controlled
boundary: GCC-Moderate
domain: cloud
canon-source: "src/uiao/canon/modernization-registry.yaml"
canon-reference:
  - "docs/customer-documents/modernization-specs/cloud/cloud.qmd"
  - "src/uiao/canon/adr/adr-059-named-commercial-exceptions.md"
derived-from: "uiao canon"
---

# Cloud Modernization Validation Suite

## Scope

Conformance tests and validation criteria for the cloud domain under
the UIAO substrate. Pairs with the
[Cloud Modernization Spec](../../../modernization-specs/cloud/cloud.qmd).
The suite gates whether a cloud-domain modernization program meets
substrate conformance — primary focus is GCC-Moderate boundary
treatment and named-exception handling per ADR-059.

[IMAGE-01: Cloud validation suite diagram. Center shows a "Boundary Conformance Gate" rectangle with the GCC-Moderate boundary line running vertically through it. Test categories enter from the left: Endpoint Resolution, Boundary Disposition, Schema Conformance, Drift Coverage. On the right, a "Pass / Fail / Exception ADR Required" outcome panel. Below the gate, a footer band lists the named exceptions per ADR-059 (Amazon Connect, SailPoint NERM). Clean engineering blueprint style, dark navy (#0D1B2E) and teal (#1E8C8C) on white background. No photographs, purely diagrammatic.]

## Test categories

### 1. Endpoint resolution conformance

Microsoft Graph adapters must:

- Resolve their endpoint via
  `uiao.adapters._graph_clouds.resolve_graph_base()`.
- Accept canonical config keys (`cloud`, `graph_api_version`,
  explicit overrides).
- Fail closed at construction for unknown clouds.
- Track API version (v1.0 / beta) explicitly per adapter.

### 2. Boundary disposition

Each cloud adapter must:

- Declare `gcc-boundary` from the canonical enum (`gcc-moderate`,
  `amazon-connect-cc`, `sailpoint-nerm`, or future ADR-authorized
  values).
- Carry the `boundary` field in every cloud-decision claim.
- Honor the `endpoint_class` field
  (`graph-v1.0 | graph-beta | arm | arc`) for boundary-aware OSCAL
  emission.

### 3. Schema conformance

- Every cloud adapter passes `schema-validation.yml` and
  `adapter-conformance.yml`.
- Cloud-decision claims validate against the cloud claim schema.
- Provenance envelope is complete on every emission.

### 4. Drift detection coverage

- `DRIFT-PROVENANCE` for boundary-crossing claims (the chain must
  remain valid; if not, the crossing must adjust
  `source_classification` to `derived`).
- `DRIFT-SCHEMA` for Graph API version transitions.
- `DRIFT-AUTHZ` for cross-boundary emissions outside declared scope.

## Evidence expectations

A conformant cloud domain produces:

- Per-claim cloud disposition records with full envelope.
- Boundary capability disposition data (which features actually
  function on each side of the boundary).
- OSCAL artifacts for cloud-related controls (SC, SI families) with
  per-claim provenance.
- Compensating-mechanism declarations where GCC-Moderate platform
  features are unavailable.

## Conformance gates

| Gate | Required | Tier |
|---|---|---|
| **T1 — endpoint resolution** | Graph adapters use canonical resolver | Required |
| **T2 — boundary disposition** | Every cloud claim carries boundary field | Required |
| **T3 — drift coverage** | Boundary-aware drift detection across all four classes | Target |
| **T4 — exception ADR coverage** | Every named exception has an authorizing ADR | Required (canon enforced) |

T4 is canon-enforced rather than test-enforced: an unauthorized
exception cannot be added to the schema enum without its ADR.

## Drift procedures

Boundary-crossing drift findings are typically `DRIFT-PROVENANCE` with
`P2` severity (1-hour SLA). They auto-remediate when deterministic
(e.g. the adapter can re-classify the claim as `derived`); otherwise
escalate to the Canon Steward or Architecture Lead.

## Honest limits

- Vendor maturity in GCC-Moderate varies; passing T1+T2 does not
  imply functional parity with commercial deployments.
- Some cloud adapter conformance tests require a live tenant; the
  CI gate runs static checks plus mocked-tenant tests.
- Named-exception authorization is canon-enforced, not test-enforced.
  Tests verify the enum is honored; ADR review is a separate process.

## Related documents

- [Modernization Spec — Cloud](../../../modernization-specs/cloud/cloud.qmd)
- [ADR-059: Named Commercial Exceptions](../../../../../src/uiao/canon/adr/adr-059-named-commercial-exceptions.md)
- [Validation Suites Index](../../../index.qmd)
